change: you change how the blacklist bit is stored in StateAccount.Extra (bit position / encoding / which mask carries blacklisted)

impact.why: Sibling write helpers that must use the same mask/encoding as IsBlacklisted; read+write of the bit must stay consistent.

The mask constant defines the bit layout; ValidateExtra rejects accounts whose Extra has bits outside AccountExtraValidMask. Changing the bit changes validity of persisted accounts (semantic: genesis/state validation).
Validates that no undefined bits are set; if the blacklist bit moves it must remain inside the valid mask or genesis/state load fails.
Reads/writes the blacklist bit on s.data.Extra via types helpers; EIP-161 empty-touch logic depends on the bit semantics.
StateDB accessors that surface the Extra bit to the rest of the node; every consumer below routes through here.
Interface contract used by the EVM and native manager; defines the behavior the bit-layout must preserve.
Native AccountManager contract that exposes set/clear/is-blacklisted to on-chain calls; relies on the StateDB bit accessors and thus on the bit layout.
EVM call path reads StateDB.IsBlacklisted for caller/from/to under Anzeon rules; consensus-affecting behavior depends on the bit being read correctly.
State transition rejects blacklisted sender/recipient/fee-payer; consensus rule riding on the bit.
Txpool admission reads State.IsBlacklisted for from/to/feePayer; mempool behavior depends on the bit.
Consensus seal verification rejects blacklisted signer via state.IsBlacklisted; consensus safety depends on the bit.

Genesis governance sync reads account.Extra with types.IsBlacklisted and writes it with types.SetBlacklisted to reconcile params vs alloc; bit-layout change reshapes genesis state and the gov-council storage set.
Genesis writes account.Extra into StateDB and validates it; the blacklist bit is part of the persisted genesis account and the genesis hash.

Serializes acc.Extra little-endian into the verkle ExtraLeafKey and decodes it back; relies on the 64-bit Extra layout / bit positions without naming IsBlacklisted. A blacklist-bit move changes the stored/committed state.
Carries Extra through slim/full account conversion used by snapshots; depends on Extra being a uint64 bitfield.
RLP encodes Extra as a uint64; the on-disk/consensus account encoding embeds the blacklist bit. Bit-layout changes alter encoded account bytes / state root.

impact.symbol: GetAccount Extra exposure / state override SetExtra
impact.why: RPC surfaces statedb.GetExtra and applies SetExtra from overrides; external clients interpret the bitfield, so a layout change is an API-visible contract change.
Tracer records pre/post Extra via GetExtra; diff output reflects the raw bitfield.

grep_would_miss: trie/verkle.go ExtraLeafKey (de)serialization — never names IsBlacklisted/blacklist, only acc.Extra; pure bit-layout dependent.
core/types/gen_account_rlp.go RLP encoding of Extra — generated, references obj.Extra not the blacklist symbol.
core/types/state_account.go SlimAccount Extra round-trip — bitfield carrier, no blacklist keyword.
AccountExtraValidMask/ValidateExtra coupling — moving the bit can flip validity without any blacklist identifier appearing at the failing call site.
internal/ethapi GetAccount Extra exposure — API contract on the raw uint64.
notes: Seed is the core/types.IsBlacklisted(uint64) bit reader (NOT systemcontracts.IsBlacklisted, which is a separate storage-set query and excluded). Real risk is semantic: the blacklist bit is a serialized part of StateAccount.Extra that flows through verkle/RLP/slim encoding and the genesis hash, plus the AccountExtraValidMask validity invariant. Test files (gov_council_test, account_manager_query_test) excluded except where they assert the encoding contract.
